name: Yamaguchi Prefecture (Japan)

colors.symbolism: the earth, tradition and steadfast character of Yamaguchi
purity and the bright future of the prefecture

description: A warm brown field charged at the centre with a white emblem stylising the kanji 山口 of the prefecture's name as a gateway through mountains.
history: Yamaguchi's prefectural symbol was adopted on 4 January 1962. The flag, with the white emblem on a brown field, has been in use as the prefectural flag since the same year.

features.symbolism: a warm earth-brown chosen to evoke the soil, traditions and steadfastness of the prefecture

features.role: Yamaguchi prefectural emblem (stylised kanji 山口)
features.symbolism: a stylised abstraction of the kanji 山口 (Yamaguchi, 'mountain pass / gateway'); the form depicts mountains opening like a gate, symbolising the prefecture as the gateway between Honshu and Kyushu.
